to Features.

I'm Priya — an aspiring Product Manager who learned to solve real problems from the ground up. Literally.

About Me

Built different.
Thinks different.

Most product managers come from engineering or business. I come from the fields of Tamil Nadu — and that's my biggest advantage.

During my B.Sc. (Hons) in Agriculture from Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, I didn't just study crops — I studied systems. I learned how soil, weather, nutrients, and human behavior interact to produce (or fail to produce) outcomes. That's product thinking in its rawest form.

When I conducted field interviews with 100+ farmers at ICRO-IPL, I wasn't doing mere data collection — I was doing user research, listening for gaps, unmet needs, and broken workflows in a high-stakes domain.

Alongside academics, I served as an NCC Cadet — earning both B and C Certificates — building discipline, leadership, and resilience that directly shapes how I work today.

Now, I'm channeling all of this into Product Management at ISBR Business School, Bengaluru — combining analytical depth with product strategy, UX thinking, and go-to-market execution.

Projects & Research

Work that Ships.

Case Study · Business Strategy
Startup Pitch — Business Case Competition (2nd Place)

The Challenge

Competed in a startup pitch event requiring a fully structured business idea — including market analysis, financial projections, and a scalable business model — presented under pressure to a live panel.

My Role

End-to-end ownership: ideation, market sizing, financial modeling, pitch narrative design, and live presentation to judges at T. John's College.

Approach

Applied structured problem-solving to develop a go-to-market strategy, competitive positioning, and revenue model. Communicated complex ideas clearly under competitive conditions.

Product Lens

Built and presented a product roadmap in competitive format — covering user need validation, solution design, business model canvas, and investor pitch narrative.

🏆

Secured 2nd Place — validating the strength of strategic thinking, structured communication, and ability to deliver impactful solutions under competitive conditions.

Research · Agriculture · Data Analysis
"Effect of Bio-Stimulants on Soil Properties & Crop Yield of Greens"

The Problem

Conventional fertilizer trials lacked structured analysis on how bio-stimulants interact with integrated nutrient management — leaving a critical evidence gap for sustainable farming decisions.

My Role

End-to-end research ownership: experimental design, field-based data collection, long-term fertilizer trial analysis, nutrient balance modeling, and structured assessment reporting.

Approach

Applied scientific methodology to isolate the effect of bio-stimulant products on soil pH, organic carbon, and crop yield metrics. Used long-term trial datasets for statistically-grounded conclusions.

Product Lens

Defined a research problem statement, designed a multi-variable experiment (akin to A/B testing), generated actionable insights from messy field data, and packaged findings for non-expert stakeholders.

🌱

Demonstrated measurable improvement in soil health metrics and yield outcomes — contributing usable data to sustainable agriculture practice guidance.

Field Research · User Interviews
Farm Input Management Gap Analysis — Amrit Internship Program

The Problem

Agricultural input companies lacked structured data on how farmers actually used fertilizers and pesticides — making it impossible to design relevant products, pricing, or outreach strategies.

My Role

Primary researcher: designed interview methodology, conducted structured interviews with 100 farmers, led large-scale data collection, and synthesized findings into an executive-level assessment report.

Approach

Employed qualitative + quantitative research: farmer interviews for depth, large-scale surveys for breadth. Identified patterns in fertilizer usage behavior, knowledge gaps, and productivity barriers.

Product Lens

Discovery research at scale — equivalent to user interviews and survey analysis in product development. Delivered a structured report with gaps, trends, and opportunities (PRD-style output from raw research).

📊

Delivered a structured assessment report across 100 data points — surfacing actionable gaps in farm input management for agri-input stakeholders.

Internship & Experience

Real world. Real impact.

Research Intern
ICRO-IPL · Coimbatore, India  |  Amrit Internship Program
